notes.MEX: Won Group A with three wins, 9 points and no goals conceded. Through as group winners, with nothing left to play.
notes.RSA: Beat South Korea 1-0 in the final game to finish 2nd on 4 points, one clear of Korea. Through as runners-up.
notes.KOR: Finished 3rd on 3 points (goal difference -1). Can't move now - they need to be one of the 8 best third-placed teams. Currently 4th of the 12, comfortably inside, but five groups finish after them, so a rush of rival wins could still push them out.
notes.CZE: Finished bottom of Group A on 1 point (-4). Eliminated.
notes.SUI: Won Group B unbeaten on 7 points. Through as group winners.
notes.CAN: Finished level with Bosnia on 4 points but took 2nd on goal difference (+5 vs -1) after the 6-0 win over Qatar. Through as runners-up.
notes.BIH: Beat Qatar 3-1 to reach 4 points and finish 3rd. Four points is strong enough that they're essentially certain to be one of the 8 best third-placed teams. Through.
notes.BRA: Finished level with Morocco on 7 points but took 1st on goal difference (+6 vs +3). Through as group winners.
notes.MAR: Level with Brazil on 7 points, 2nd on goal difference. Through as runners-up.
notes.SCO: Finished 3rd on 3 points but with goal difference -3, the weakest of the teams on 3 points. Currently 7th of the 12 third-placed teams, just inside the 8. Any third-placed team that finishes on 4 points, or on 3 with a better goal difference, pushes Scotland toward the cut line.
notes.HAI: Lost all three games, 0 points (-6). Eliminated.
notes.USA: Top of Group D on 6 points (+5) and already through. Win or draw against Türkiye and they finish 1st. They'd only drop to 2nd by losing while the Paraguay-Australia winner overturns a 5-goal swing.
notes.AUS: On 3 points and 2nd on goal difference (0 vs Paraguay's -2). Win or draw against Paraguay and they're through in 2nd. A defeat drops them to 3rd on 3 points and almost certainly out.
notes.PAR: On 3 points (-2). Must beat Australia to take 2nd outright. A draw leaves them 3rd on 4 points, alive only as a possible best third-placed team. A defeat leaves them 3rd on 3 points and almost certainly out.
notes.GER: Top of Group E on 6 points (+7) and already through. A win or draw against Ecuador secures 1st.
notes.CIV: On 3 points and 2nd. Beat Curaçao, or draw, and they're through in 2nd. A defeat drops them to 3rd and reliant on a wildcard.
notes.ECU: On 1 point. Must beat Germany to reach 4 points, and even then need Ivory Coast to lose to Curaçao or to overhaul Ivory Coast on goal difference. A draw or defeat eliminates them.
notes.CUW: On 1 point (-6). Must beat Ivory Coast to reach 4 points and also need Ecuador to lose; their -6 goal difference makes a wildcard place virtually impossible. A draw or defeat eliminates them.
notes.NED: Top of Group F on 4 points (+4), ahead of Japan on goals scored. A draw against Tunisia guarantees they go through; a win secures 1st.
notes.JPN: On 4 points (+4), level with the Netherlands but 2nd on goals scored. A win or draw against Sweden sends them through; they win the group by beating the Dutch result.
notes.SWE: On 3 points. Beat Japan and they reach 6 points and go through. A draw leaves them 3rd on 4 points, needing a wildcard; a defeat eliminates them.
notes.EGY: Top of Group G on 4 points (+2). Beat Iran and they win the group; a draw still sends them through unless Belgium win and overturn their goal difference.
notes.IRN: On 2 points, 2nd on goals scored. Beat Egypt and they're through. A draw leaves them needing Belgium to slip up; a defeat sends them out.
notes.BEL: On 2 points and currently 9th of the 12 third-placed teams - just below the cut. Beat New Zealand and they reach 5 points and almost certainly go through. A draw (3 points) leaves them needing a wildcard.
notes.NZL: On 1 point. Must beat Belgium to reach 4 points and stay alive. A draw or defeat eliminates them.
notes.ESP: Top of Group H on 4 points (+4) with no goals conceded. A draw against Uruguay secures it; they'd only fall to 3rd by losing while Cape Verde win and overturn a 4-goal swing.
notes.URU: On 2 points. Beat Spain and they're through. A draw (3 points) leaves them dependent on the Cape Verde result; a defeat almost certainly eliminates them.
notes.CPV: On 2 points and currently holding the last wildcard spot (8th of 12) on goals scored. Beat Saudi Arabia and they reach 5 points and are safely through. A draw keeps them in the race; a defeat likely knocks them out.
notes.KSA: On 1 point (-4). Must beat Cape Verde to reach 4 points and have any chance. A draw or defeat eliminates them.
notes.FRA: On 6 points (+5) and already through. Win or draw against Norway and they finish 1st, leading on goal difference (+5 vs +4).
notes.NOR: On 6 points (+4) and already through. Must beat France to win the group; any other result and they finish 2nd.
notes.SEN: On 0 points. Must beat Iraq to reach 3 points and finish 3rd, but their goal difference (around -2) would be among the worst third-placed teams, so a wildcard is highly unlikely. A draw or defeat eliminates them.
notes.IRQ: On 0 points (-6). Must beat Senegal to reach 3 points and finish 3rd; even then a goal difference around -5 makes a wildcard extremely unlikely. A draw or defeat eliminates them.
notes.ARG: Top of Group J on 6 points (+5) and already through. Win or draw against Jordan and they finish 1st.
notes.AUT: On 3 points and 2nd on goal difference (0 vs Algeria's -2). Win or draw against Algeria and they go through in 2nd. A defeat drops them to 3rd and reliant on a wildcard.
notes.ALG: On 3 points (-2). Must beat Austria to take 2nd. A draw leaves them 3rd on 4 points, chasing a wildcard; a defeat almost certainly eliminates them.
notes.COL: Top of Group K on 6 points and already through. Win or draw against Portugal and they finish 1st.
notes.POR: On 4 points (+5) and all but through. Beat Colombia and they win the group; any other result and they finish 2nd or, at worst, take a wildcard.
notes.COD: On 1 point. Must beat Uzbekistan to reach 4 points and finish 3rd, which would put them in the wildcard race. A draw or defeat eliminates them.
notes.ENG: Top of Group L on 4 points (+2). Beat Panama and they win the group; a draw also tops it unless Ghana win and overturn their goal difference.
notes.GHA: On 4 points (+1) with one goal conceded all group. A win or draw against Croatia sends them through.
notes.CRO: On 3 points (-1). Beat Ghana and they reach 6 points and go through. A draw leaves them 3rd on 4 points, needing a wildcard; a defeat almost certainly eliminates them.
notes.PAN: Lost both games, 0 points, no goals scored. Eliminated.

stakes.D-4: USA (6 pts, +5) have qualified and finish 1st with a win or a draw - they'd only fall to 2nd by losing while the other game's winner overturns a 5-goal swing. Türkiye (0 pts) are eliminated.
stakes.D-5: Both on 3 points, with Australia 2nd on goal difference (0 vs -2). Australia go through with a win or a draw. Paraguay must win to take 2nd; a draw leaves them 3rd on 4 points and chasing a wildcard; a defeat ends it.
stakes.E-4: Ivory Coast (3 pts) go through in 2nd with a win or a draw. Curaçao (1 pt, -6) must win and also need Ecuador to lose - and even then their goal difference makes a wildcard almost impossible.
stakes.E-5: Germany (6 pts, +7) secure 1st with a win or a draw. Ecuador (1 pt) must win to reach 4 points, and then need Ivory Coast to lose or to pass them on goal difference. A draw or defeat sends Ecuador out.
stakes.F-4: Japan (4 pts) go through with a win or a draw. Sweden (3 pts) must win to reach 6 and qualify; a draw leaves them 3rd on 4 points, needing a wildcard; a defeat eliminates them.
stakes.F-5: Netherlands (4 pts, +4) qualify with a draw and win the group with a victory. Tunisia (0 pts, -8) are eliminated.
stakes.G-4: Egypt (4 pts) win the group with a victory and qualify with a draw. Iran (2 pts) reach the top two with a win; a draw leaves them needing Belgium to drop points; a defeat sends them out.
stakes.G-5: Belgium (2 pts) sit just below the wildcard line - a win takes them to 5 points and almost certainly through; a draw leaves them sweating on a wildcard. New Zealand (1 pt) must win to stay alive.
stakes.H-4: Spain (4 pts, +4) qualify with a draw and win the group with a victory. Uruguay (2 pts) must win to be sure; a draw leaves them on 3 points and dependent on the other result; a defeat all but eliminates them.
stakes.H-5: Cape Verde (2 pts) currently hold the last wildcard spot - a win takes them to 5 and safely through. Saudi Arabia (1 pt, -4) must win to reach 4 points and survive; anything else eliminates them.
stakes.I-4: France and Norway (both 6 pts) have already qualified; this decides the winner. France finish 1st with a win or a draw (ahead on goal difference); Norway must win to top the group.
stakes.I-5: Senegal and Iraq are both on 0 points. The winner reaches 3 points and 3rd place, but with a goal difference among the worst third-placed teams a wildcard is highly unlikely. A draw eliminates both.
stakes.J-4: Level on 3 points, with Austria 2nd on goal difference (0 vs -2). Austria go through with a win or a draw. Algeria must win to take 2nd; a draw leaves them 3rd on 4 points, chasing a wildcard.
stakes.J-5: Argentina (6 pts, +5) finish 1st with a win or a draw. Jordan (0 pts) are eliminated.
stakes.K-4: Colombia (6 pts) and Portugal (4 pts, +5) are both effectively through. Colombia win the group with a win or a draw; Portugal must win to finish 1st.
stakes.K-5: DR Congo (1 pt) must win to reach 4 points and finish 3rd, entering the wildcard race. A draw or defeat eliminates them. Uzbekistan (0 pts, -7) are out.
stakes.L-4: England (4 pts, +2) win the group with a victory, or with a draw if Ghana don't win. Panama (0 pts) are eliminated.
stakes.L-5: Ghana (4 pts) go through with a win or a draw. Croatia (3 pts) must win to reach 6 and qualify; a draw leaves them 3rd on 4 points, needing a wildcard; a defeat all but eliminates them.
